response.justification: The candidate has a long career in web and full-stack software development with some low-latency systems work, but has no discernible experience in any of the core competencies this role demands: SoC/IP modeling, GPU or AI accelerator architecture, deep learning workload optimization, or hardware-software co-design. The role sits at the intersection of computer architecture and ML systems engineering — a domain entirely absent from this resume. The only tangentially relevant experience is low-latency service development at Sociomantic, which is orders of magnitude removed from the hardware-level performance work required here.
strengths: Long career (~17 years) demonstrating consistent growth and adaptability across diverse technical domains
Demonstrated performance-oriented systems work at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and low-latency techniques
Exposure to systems-level languages (C, C++, DLang) indicating comfort below the application layer
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator design — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep learning, neural network training/inference, or AI workload analysis of any kind
Career trajectory is firmly web/full-stack; there is no evidence of a pivot toward hardware or ML systems engineering

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
Some low-latency and systems programming exposure
